name: Jerry Wexler
abstract: Gerald Wexler (January 10, 1917 – August 15, 2008) was a music journalist turned music producer, and was a major influence on American popular music from the 1950s through the 1980s. He coined the term "rhythm and blues", and was integral in signing and/or producing many of the biggest acts of the time, including Ray Charles, the Allman …

about_quotes.speaker: Cosimo Matassa
about_quotes.evidence: Interviewer mentions Jerry Wexler in the context of publishing rights and Alan Freed; Interviewee responds about the song existing before Wexler recorded it, but does not describe a personal or professional relationship with Wexler himself.

about_quotes.speaker: Cosimo Matassa
about_quotes.evidence: So, you didn't have to tell Ahmed Erdogan, for instance, or Jerry Wexler what to do... But, but they were, they were a great team... Like at the end of the session... I appreciated that in them.

about_quotes.speaker: Tommy Ridgley
about_quotes.evidence: I used to call Jerry Wexler. I'd get on my phone and call Jerry Wexler. Me and him talk just like we talk.

about_quotes.speaker: Joel Dorn
about_quotes.evidence: I got in hassles with Wexler, I got in hassles with Ahmet, you know.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joel Dorn interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Wallace Davenport
about_quotes.evidence: No, no, that name sounds familiar, I'm trying to picture who he is.

about_quotes.speaker: Dick Hyman
about_quotes.evidence: I played a lot of stuff for Atlantic Records — Jerry Wexler and —
about_quotes.doc_title: Dick Hyman interviewed by Monk Rowe, Scottsdale, Arizona, March 4, 1995 [interview 1 of 2]
